get.description:
Retrieve a list of accounts associated with your workspace.

This endpoint returns all connected accounts including their platform, username, and current status.

Response:
- Returns an array of account objects
- Each account includes id, platform, username, and status

Retrieve account usage statistics across all connected platforms.

This endpoint is used to get the total number of connected accounts along with a breakdown per platform.
It is typically used for monitoring account usage and checking limits based on the current plan.

Response:
- Returns an object containing total accounts and count per platform
- Includes platform breakdown such as Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, Threads, and LinkedIn
- Includes account limit information

Retrieve detailed information for a specific account.

Provide the account ID to get full details including profile information, platform data, and connection status.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The unique identifier of the account

Response:
- Returns a single account object
- Includes detailed information such as id, platform, username, status, and additional metadata

delete.description:
Remove a connected account from the system.

This endpoint is used to disconnect and delete an account from your workspace.
Once removed, the account will no longer be accessible and cannot be used for any operations such as fetching content, comments, or posting.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The unique identifier of the account

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Retrieve a list of comments stored in the system.

This endpoint is used to fetch comments that have been collected and stored in Repliz (previously known as queue).
It includes comments from various platforms that are ready for management, moderation, or automation.

Response:
- Returns an array of comment objects
- Each comment includes id, platform, content, status, and timestamp

content.title: Tiga Permintaan Nabi Sulaiman Setelah Membangun Masjid Al-Aqsa Kisah Nabi Sulaiman
content.description:
Rasulullah menyebutkan tiga permohonan yang diajukan Nabi Sulaiman setelah membangun Baitul Maqdis (Masjid Al-Aqsa): hukum yang sesuai hukum Allah kerajaan yang tidak dimiliki orang lain dan pengampunan dosa bagi yang salat di sana.
Video Lengkap Cek Bio

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information of a specific stored comment.

This endpoint is used to get complete data of a single comment, including its content, platform source, and current status.
It is typically used before performing actions such as replying or updating status.

Parameters:
- commentId (string) — The unique identifier of the comment

Response:
- Returns a single comment object with detailed information

content.title: Tiga Permintaan Nabi Sulaiman Setelah Membangun Masjid Al-Aqsa Kisah Nabi Sulaiman
content.description:
Rasulullah menyebutkan tiga permohonan yang diajukan Nabi Sulaiman setelah membangun Baitul Maqdis (Masjid Al-Aqsa): hukum yang sesuai hukum Allah kerajaan yang tidak dimiliki orang lain dan pengampunan dosa bagi yang salat di sana.
Video Lengkap Cek Bio

post.description:
Send a reply to a stored comment.

This endpoint is used to respond to a comment that exists in the system.
The reply will be sent to the original platform where the comment was created.

Parameters:
- commentId (string) — The unique identifier of the comment

Response:
- Returns the created reply object
- Includes id, message, and timestamp

put.description:
Update the status of a stored comment.

This endpoint is used to manage the lifecycle of a comment, such as marking it as resolved, ignored, or pending.
It helps organize and track comment handling within the system.

Parameters:
- commentId (string) — The unique identifier of the comment
- status (string) — The updated status of the comment

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Retrieve a list of currently music from TikTok platforms.

This endpoint is used to get popular or trending audio that can be used for content creation, especially on platforms like Instagram or other short-video services.
It helps users discover music that is currently gaining engagement.

Response:
- Returns an array of music objects
- Each item includes id, title, artist, and usage metadata

Retrieve product information from Shopee.

This endpoint is used to fetch Shopee product details using a product URL or identifier.
It can be used to display product information before attaching or publishing content related to the product.

Response:
- Returns product information from Shopee
- Includes product details such as id, name, and image

Retrieve metadata information from a given URL.

This endpoint is used to extract preview data from a link, such as title, description, thumbnail, and other relevant metadata.
It is typically used to generate link previews before sharing or posting content.

Parameters:
- url (string) — The target URL to extract metadata from

Response:
- Returns metadata object
- Includes title, description, thumbnail, and additional link information

example.title: Repliz – Smart Comment Management for All Platforms
example.description: Repliz helps you manage comments from YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok and LinkedIn – all in one app. Automate replies, filter spam, and save time.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of scheduled posts.

This endpoint is used to fetch all scheduled content that will be published to connected platforms.
It includes information such as scheduled time, content type, and current status.

Response:
- Returns an array of schedule objects
- Each schedule includes id, platform, type, scheduled time, and status

meta.title: Repliz – Smart Comment Management for All Platforms
meta.description: Repliz helps you manage comments from YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok and LinkedIn – all in one app. Automate replies, filter spam, and save time.

post.description:
Create a new scheduled post.

This endpoint is used to schedule content publishing to supported platforms at a specified time.

Supported Post Types:
- Text → Facebook, Threads
- Image → Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok, LinkedIn
- Video → Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok, YouTube, LinkedIn
- Reels → Facebook
- Album → Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok, LinkedIn
- Link → Facebook
- Story → Facebook, Instagram

Response:
- Returns the created scheduleId

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information of a specific schedule.

This endpoint is used to get complete data of a scheduled post, including content, platform, and execution details.

Parameters:
- scheduleId (string) — The unique identifier of the schedule

Response:
- Returns a single schedule object with detailed information

meta.title: Repliz – Smart Comment Management for All Platforms
meta.description: Repliz helps you manage comments from YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok and LinkedIn – all in one app. Automate replies, filter spam, and save time.

put.description:
Update an existing scheduled post.

This endpoint is used to modify scheduled content, including its content, schedule time, or target platform.

Supported Post Types:
- Text → Facebook, Threads
- Image → Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok, LinkedIn
- Video → Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok, YouTube, LinkedIn
- Reels → Facebook
- Album → Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok, LinkedIn
- Link → Facebook
- Story → Facebook, Instagram

Parameters:
- scheduleId (string) — The unique identifier of the schedule

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

delete.description:
Delete a scheduled post.

This endpoint is used to remove a schedule so it will not be executed.

Parameters:
- scheduleId (string) — The unique identifier of the schedule

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

put.description:
Retry a failed scheduled post.

This endpoint is used to reprocess a schedule that previously failed to publish.
It will attempt to execute the schedule again using the existing data.

Parameters:
- scheduleId (string) — The unique identifier of the schedule

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

delete.description:
Delete multiple scheduled posts.

This endpoint is used to remove multiple schedules at once so they will not be executed.

Parameters:
- scheduleIds (string[]) — Array of schedule identifiers to be removed

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Retrieve a list of chats associated with your account.

This endpoint returns all available chats including basic information such as last message, participant, and chat status.
It is typically used to display chat lists in an application.

Response:
- Returns an array of chat objects
- Each chat includes id, participant info, last message, and status

Retrieve detailed information of a specific chat.

This endpoint is used to get full chat details including participant data and chat metadata.
It is commonly used when opening a specific conversation.

Parameters:
- chatId (string) — The unique identifier of the chat

Response:
- Returns a single chat object with detailed information

Retrieve messages from a specific chat.

This endpoint returns a list of messages within a chat, including sender, content, and timestamp.
It supports fetching message history for displaying conversations.

Parameters:
- chatId (string) — The unique identifier of the chat

Response:
- Returns an array of message objects
- Each message includes id, sender, content, and timestamp

post.description:
Send a message to a specific chat.

This endpoint is used to send a new message within a conversation.
The message can include text or other supported content types.

Parameters:
- chatId (string) — The unique identifier of the chat

Response:
- Returns the sent message object
- Includes id, content, sender, and timestamp

post.description:
Mark a chat as read.

This endpoint is used to update the read status of a chat, indicating that all messages have been seen.
It is typically triggered when a user opens or views a conversation.

Parameters:
- chatId (string) — The unique identifier of the chat

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Retrieve a list of content directly from connected platforms such as YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, Threads and LinkedIn.

This endpoint is used to fetch available content (e.g. posts, videos, media) along with basic information such as title, caption, and publish date.
It is typically used for displaying content lists or selecting content for further operations.

Response:
- Returns an array of content objects
- Each content includes id, platform, title/caption, and publish timestamp

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information of a specific content from the platform.

This endpoint is used to get complete data of a single content item, including metadata such as description, statistics, and media details.

Parameters:
- contentId (string) — The unique identifier of the content

Response:
- Returns a single content object with detailed information

example.title: Tiga Permintaan Nabi Sulaiman Setelah Membangun Masjid Al-Aqsa Kisah Nabi Sulaiman
example.description:
Rasulullah menyebutkan tiga permohonan yang diajukan Nabi Sulaiman setelah membangun Baitul Maqdis (Masjid Al-Aqsa): hukum yang sesuai hukum Allah kerajaan yang tidak dimiliki orang lain dan pengampunan dosa bagi yang salat di sana.
Video Lengkap Cek Bio

get.description:
Retrieve comments from a specific content directly from the platform.

This endpoint returns a list of comments including user information, message, and timestamp.
It is commonly used to display comment threads or perform moderation.

Parameters:
- contentId (string) — The unique identifier of the content

Response:
- Returns an array of comment objects
- Each comment includes id, username, message, and timestamp

post.description:
Create a new comment on a specific content in the platform.

This endpoint is used to post a comment directly to the platform under a selected content item.

Parameters:
- contentId (string) — The unique identifier of the content

Response:
- Returns the created comment object
- Includes id, message, author, and timestamp

get.description:
Retrieve engagement statistics for a specific content directly from the platform.

This endpoint is used to fetch insight metrics such as likes, comments, shares, views, and other platform-specific engagement data.
The returned fields may vary depending on the platform (e.g. Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, etc).

Parameters:
- contentId (string) — The unique identifier of the content

Response:
- Returns a statistic object based on the platform
- Includes engagement metrics such as like, comment, share, views, and other platform-specific fields

post.description:
Reply to an existing comment on a specific content.

This endpoint is used to send a reply to a comment, allowing interaction within comment threads directly on the platform.

Parameters:
- commentId (string) — The unique identifier of the comment

Response:
- Returns the created reply object
- Includes id, message, author, and timestamp

value.text: Hello there, this is from Repliz

button.text: Hello there, this is from Repliz
Hello there, this is from Repliz

delete.description:
Delete a comment from a specific content on the platform.

This endpoint is used to remove a comment, typically for moderation purposes.

Parameters:
- commentId (string) — The unique identifier of the comment

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Search public content on Threads.

This endpoint is used to search Threads posts based on a keyword or phrase.
It allows retrieving content from the platform for discovery, analysis, or engagement purposes.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The connected Threads account identifier
- search (string) — Keyword or phrase used to search content
- nextToken (string, optional) — Token for retrieving the next page of results

Response:
- Returns an array of Threads content objects
- Includes content details such as id, text, media, author, and publish timestamp
- May include nextToken for pagination

Retrieve Threads content from a specific user.

This endpoint is used to fetch posts from a target Threads user by username.
It is useful for monitoring, reviewing, or analyzing content from a specific account.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The connected Threads account identifier
- username (string) — The target Threads username
- nextToken (string, optional) — Token for retrieving the next page of results

Response:
- Returns an array of Threads content objects from the specified user
- Includes content details such as id, text, media, and publish timestamp
- May include nextToken for pagination

Search for Threads users by username.

This endpoint is used to find public Threads accounts matching the provided username.
It helps identify users before retrieving their content.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The connected Threads account identifier
- username (string) — The target Threads username to search

Response:
- Returns an array of user objects
- Includes user details such as id, username, display name, and profile information

Initiate Facebook authorization process.

This endpoint is used to redirect users to Facebook OAuth authorization page.
After successful authorization, the user will be redirected back to the provided redirect URL with an authorization code.

Parameters:
- redirect (string) — The URL to redirect after authorization is completed

Response:
- Redirects user to Facebook OAuth page

Retrieve Facebook pages associated with the authorized account.

This endpoint is used to fetch a list of pages that the user has access to.
It requires an access token obtained from the exchange process.

Parameters:
- token (string) — Access token obtained from the exchange endpoint

Response:
- Returns an array of page objects
- Each page includes id, name, and access information

post.description:
Exchange authorization code for an access token.

This endpoint is used after the authorization step to convert the received code into a usable access token.

Parameters:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from Facebook redirect

Response:
- Returns an access token used for further Facebook API requests

Connect a Facebook page to the system.

This endpoint is used to link a selected Facebook page into the platform using the page ID and access token.

Body:
- pageId (string) — The selected Facebook page ID
- token (string) — Access token associated with the selected page

Response:
- Returns the connected account id information

Reconnect an existing Facebook account.

This endpoint is used when a previously connected account needs to be reauthorized (e.g. expired token).
It requires a new token and the selected page ID.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The unique identifier of the account

Body:
- pageId (string) — The selected Facebook page ID
- token (string) — New access token obtained from exchange

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Initiate Instagram authorization process.

This endpoint is used to redirect users to Instagram OAuth authorization page.
After successful authorization, the user will be redirected back to the provided redirect URL with an authorization code.

Parameters:
- redirect (string) — The URL to redirect after authorization is completed

Response:
- Redirects user to Instagram OAuth page

post.description:
Connect an Instagram account to the system.

This endpoint is used to complete the connection process using the authorization code obtained from the redirect callback.
The system will exchange the code and link the Instagram account.

Body:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from Instagram redirect

Response:
- Returns the connected account id information

Reconnect an existing Instagram account.

This endpoint is used when a previously connected account needs to be reauthorized (e.g. expired session).
It requires a new authorization code from the Instagram callback.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The unique identifier of the account

Body:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from Instagram redirect

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Initiate Threads authorization process.

This endpoint is used to redirect users to Threads OAuth authorization page.
After successful authorization, the user will be redirected back to the provided redirect URL with an authorization code.

Parameters:
- redirect (string) — The URL to redirect after authorization is completed

Response:
- Redirects user to Threads OAuth page

post.description:
Connect a Threads account to the system.

This endpoint is used to complete the connection process using the authorization code obtained from the redirect callback.
The system will exchange the code and link the Threads account.

Body:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from Threads redirect

Response:
- Returns the connected account id information

Reconnect an existing Threads account.

This endpoint is used when a previously connected account needs to be reauthorized (e.g. expired session).
It requires a new authorization code from the Threads callback.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The unique identifier of the account

Body:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from Threads redirect

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Initiate YouTube authorization process.

This endpoint is used to redirect users to Google OAuth authorization page for YouTube access.
After successful authorization, the user will be redirected back to the provided redirect URL with an authorization code.

Parameters:
- redirect (string) — The URL to redirect after authorization is completed

Response:
- Redirects user to Google OAuth page

Retrieve YouTube channels associated with the authorized account.

This endpoint is used to fetch a list of channels that the user has access to.
It requires an access token obtained from the exchange process.

Parameters:
- token (string) — Access token obtained from the exchange endpoint

Response:
- Returns an array of channel objects
- Each channel includes id, name, and basic channel information

post.description:
Exchange authorization code for an access token.

This endpoint is used after the authorization step to convert the received code into a usable access token.

Parameters:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from the redirect

Response:
- Returns an access token used for YouTube API requests

Connect a YouTube channel to the system.

This endpoint is used to link a selected YouTube channel using the channel ID and access token.

Body:
- channelId (string) — The selected YouTube channel ID
- token (string) — Access token associated with the channel

Response:
- Returns the connected account id information

Reconnect an existing YouTube account.

This endpoint is used when a previously connected account needs to be reauthorized (e.g. expired token).
It requires a new token and the selected channel ID.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The unique identifier of the account

Body:
- channelId (string) — The selected YouTube channel ID
- token (string) — New access token obtained from exchange

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Initiate LinkedIn authorization process.

This endpoint is used to redirect users to LinkedIn OAuth authorization page.
After successful authorization, the user will be redirected back to the provided redirect URL with an authorization code.

Parameters:
- redirect (string) — The URL to redirect after authorization is completed

Response:
- Redirects user to LinkedIn OAuth page

Retrieve LinkedIn organizations associated with the authorized account.

This endpoint is used to fetch a list of organizations that the user has access to.
It requires an access token obtained from the exchange process.

Parameters:
- token (string) — Access token obtained from the exchange endpoint

Response:
- Returns an array of organization objects
- Each organization includes id, name, and access information

post.description:
Exchange authorization code for an access token.

This endpoint is used after the authorization step to convert the received code into a usable access token.

Parameters:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from LinkedIn redirect

Response:
- Returns an access token used for further LinkedIn API requests

Connect a LinkedIn organization to the system.

This endpoint is used to link a selected LinkedIn organization using the organization ID and access token.

Body:
- organizationId (string) — The selected LinkedIn organization ID
- token (string) — Access token associated with the organization

Response:
- Returns the connected account id information

Reconnect an existing LinkedIn account.

This endpoint is used when a previously connected account needs to be reauthorized (e.g. expired token).
It requires a new token and the selected organization ID.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The unique identifier of the account

Body:
- organizationId (string) — The selected LinkedIn organization ID
- token (string) — New access token obtained from exchange

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Initiate TikTok authorization process.

This endpoint is used to redirect users to TikTok OAuth authorization page.
After successful authorization, the user will be redirected back to the provided redirect URL with an authorization code.

Parameters:
- redirect (string) — The URL to redirect after authorization is completed

Response:
- Redirects user to TikTok OAuth page

post.description:
Connect a TikTok account to the system.

This endpoint is used to complete the connection process using the authorization code obtained from the redirect callback.
The system will exchange the code and link the TikTok account.

Body:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from TikTok redirect

Response:
- Returns the connected account id information

Reconnect an existing TikTok account.

This endpoint is used when a previously connected account needs to be reauthorized (e.g. expired session).
It requires a new authorization code from the TikTok callback.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The unique identifier of the account

Body:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from TikTok redirect

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

get.description:
Initiate Shopee authorization process.

This endpoint is used to redirect users to Shopee authorization page.
After successful authorization, the user will be redirected back to the provided redirect URL with an authorization code.

Parameters:
- redirect (string) — The URL to redirect after authorization is completed

Response:
- Redirects user to Shopee authorization page

post.description:
Connect a Shopee account to the system.

This endpoint is used to complete the connection process using the authorization code obtained from the redirect callback.
The system will exchange the code and link the Shopee account.

Body:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from Shopee redirect

Response:
- Returns the connected account information

Reconnect an existing Shopee account.

This endpoint is used when a previously connected account needs to be reauthorized (e.g. expired session).
It requires a new authorization code from the Shopee callback.

Parameters:
- accountId (string) — The unique identifier of the account

Body:
- code (string) — Authorization code received from Shopee redirect

Response:
- Returns the updated account connection status

get.description:
Retrieve storage usage statistics.

This endpoint is used to get information about the current storage usage, including the total number of files, used storage space, and remaining available capacity.

Response:
- Returns storage usage statistics
- Includes information such as total files, used storage, and storage limit

Retrieve a list of uploaded files.

This endpoint is used to fetch files stored in your storage.
It supports pagination and optional search by filename.

Parameters:
- page (number) — The page number to retrieve
- limit (number) — Number of files per page
- search (string, optional) — Search files by filename

Response:
- Returns an array of file objects
- Each file includes id, filename, size, mimetype, and upload information

Retrieve detailed information of a specific file.

This endpoint is used to fetch complete information about a stored file.

Parameters:
- fileId (string) — The unique identifier of the file

Response:
- Returns a single file object with detailed metadata

delete.description:
Delete a stored file.

This endpoint is used to permanently remove a file from storage.

Parameters:
- fileId (string) — The unique identifier of the file

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

post.description:
Initialize a file upload.

This endpoint is used before uploading a file to storage.
Provide the filename, file size, and MIME type to create an upload session.

Body:
- filename (string) — Name of the file
- size (number) — File size in bytes
- mimetype (string) — MIME type of the file

Response:
- Returns upload session information
- Includes the file identifier and upload URL required to upload the file

Complete a file upload.

This endpoint is used after the file has been successfully uploaded to storage.
It finalizes the upload process and makes the file available for use.

Parameters:
- fileId (string) — The unique identifier of the uploaded file

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

delete.description:
Delete multiple files.

This endpoint is used to remove multiple files at once so they will not be executed.

Parameters:
- fileIds (string[]) — Array of file identifiers to be removed

Response:
- No content is returned on success (204 No Content)

info.title: Repliz API
info.description:
Repliz Public API allows developers to securely access and integrate core features such as comment management, automation, analytics, and AI-powered interactions into their own applications.
This API is designed for external usage, enabling seamless communication with Repliz services in a scalable and efficient way.

Authentication
All requests to the Public API must be authenticated using HTTP Basic Auth.

- Access Key is used as the username
- Secret Key is used as the password

Example:
Authorization: Basic base64(accessKey:secretKey).

Make sure to keep your credentials secure and do not expose them in client-side applications. All endpoints require authentication unless stated otherwise.

title.example: Repliz – Smart Comment Management for All Platforms

description.example: Repliz helps you manage comments from YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok and LinkedIn – all in one app. Automate replies, filter spam, and save time.
